title: Pricing rollback after churn spike
description: A pricing change caused a 15% churn spike in one segment. Decide whether to roll back, adjust, or hold firm, and write the customer comms.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at LedgerSpring, a Series B fintech company (165 employees, $14.8M ARR). Your immediate assignment is pricing rollback after churn spike. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Make trade-offs explicit so every team understands what we are not doing.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 70994
New logo adds: 2714
Activation rate: 47%
30-day retention: 55%
NPS: 30 (n=113)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 12%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 5 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 4 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Farah Hayes): "I need a plan sales can explain in live calls next week, not a roadmap promise."
VP Engineering (Owen Brooks): "We can move quickly if every blocker has an owner and fallback path."
Head of Customer Success (Elena Klein): "Trust loss shows up before churn in expansion conversations."
Commercial note: 6 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 49 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Ridgepoint Media (Operations Lead): "This solved one team workflow immediately, then stalled when we tried to scale."
Pinecrest Retail (IT Manager): "A few avoidable errors in onboarding made the team question readiness."
Summit Manufacturing (Director of Product): "Give us a narrower path that works consistently and we will scale internally."
Data gap: limited qualitative input from accounts that never activated.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 45
Median cycle time: 16 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 59%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 8
Release rollback events last quarter: 3
Average time to resolve escalations: 59 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 47% and retention is 55%, while NPS (30, n=113) and 49 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Farah Hayes around the 6 deal pipeline, technical caution from Owen Brooks on dependency risk (8 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Elena Klein due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 70994 active accounts, activation 47%, retention 55%, and on-time delivery 59%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
